rulings.Case Title: STEPHANIE BERG et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 1, 2007, LINE 4, DEFENDANT BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE LELAND STANFORD JUNIOR'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE FAILS TO MEET BAHL BECAUSE COUNSEL HAS FAILED TO PROVIDE ANY FACTUAL BASIS THAT SUGGESTS DOCUMENTS OR EVIDENCE EXIST TO OPPOSE THE MOTION. THE DECLARATION IS SILENT AS TO PLANT INSULATION DISCOVERY TAKEN IN THIS OR OTHER CASES, AND THE COURT NOTES THAT PLAINTIFF HAS FILED NUMEROUS SUITS AGAINST PLANT INSULATION. PLAINTIFF ALSO HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT PLAINTIFF CAN PRESENT ANY EVIDENCE ON PLAINTIFF'S BEHALF AS TO WHAT WORK DECEDENT PERFORMED AT STANFORD GIVEN THAT NO CO WORKERS WILL TESTIFY AND DECEDENT'S TESTIMONY DOES NOT APPEAR TO BE ADMISSISBLE AS TO DEFENDANT. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: EDWIN RICHARDSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 1, 2007, LINE 8, DEFENDANT DURAMETTALIC CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DENY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, FAILED TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. WELCH DECLARATION IS NOT ADMISSIBLE FOR LACK OF FOUNDATION. PLAINTIFF'S DISCOVERY IS NOT FACTUALLY DEVOID. PLAINTIFF WAS EXPOSED TO DEFENDANT'S PRODUCT AND DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT PRODUCT DID NOT CONTAIN ASBESTOS. GRANT SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON FRAUD AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIM WHERE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: LEONORE DEATON VS. 16TH & DELORES ASSOCIATES L.P. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Deft'S Special Motion To Strike Complaint;Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 1, 2007, LINE 17, DEFENDANT 16TH & DELORES ASSOCIATES L.P.'S Special Motion To Strike Complaint. THE MOTION IS DENIED. CCP SECTION 425.16 MAY BE INVOKED AS IT RELATES TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION AND POSSIBLY THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE OF THE RELIANCE ON THE NOTICE OF TERMINATION. HOWEVER, THE THRUST OF THE ENTIRE COMPLAINT IS ON PLAINTIFF'S RIGHT TO QUIET ENJOYMENT AND HABITABLE PREMISES. TO THE EXTENT CCP SECTION 425.16 IS APPLICABLE, PLAINTIFF HAS ESTABLISHED A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING BY POINTING THE EXISTENCE OF FACTS IN SUPPORT OF HER CLAIM BY DECLARATION AND BY REFERENCE TO DOCUMENTS WHICH CERTAINLY WILL BE ADMISSIBLE AT THE TIME OF TRIAL. TO THE EXTENT EVIDENCE HAS BEEN PRESENTED IN OPPOSITION THAT MAY NOT CONFORM TO ALL OF THE REQUIREMENTS OF THE EVIDENCE CODE, IT IS OBVIOUS THAT PLAINTIFF CAN OVERCOME THE OBJECTIONS TO EXHIBITS D, E, F, I, J, K. THE DECLARATIONS, EXHIBITS A, B AND C ARE ADMISSIBLE. =(302/PJM/SN-X)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES EHRLICH et al VS. MYCELIA NETWORKS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs James Ehrlich And Douglas Arrison'S Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 1, 2007, LINE 18, Plaintiffs James Ehrlich And Douglas Arrison's Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS HAVE FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THEY ARE LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THEIR CLAIM. THE FINAL PAYMENT FOR THE SALE OF MYCELIA IS NOT UNTIL AUGUST 2008 AND THERE IS AMPLE TIME FOR THE PARTIES TO ENGAGE IN DISCOVERY AND MOVE FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT TO PROVE THEIR CLAIM. IF THEY DO SO, INJUNCTIVE RELIEF MAY THEN BE APPROPRIATE. =(302/PJM/AY)
